left-right-join.label: LEFT and RIGHT JOIN
left-right-join.summary: Include unmatched rows with LEFT and RIGHT JOIN.

sketch0.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?

sketch1.title: Why use LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?

sk1.title: What are LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?
sk1.bodyMarkdown: LEFT JOIN and RIGHT JOIN are types of SQL joins that allow you to combine rows from two tables, including rows that do not have matching values in the other table. A LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the left table and the matched rows from the right table. If there is no match, the result is NULL on the right side. A RIGHT JOIN does the opposite: it returns all rows from the right table and matched rows from the left table, with NULLs where there is no match.

sk2.title: Why use LEFT and RIGHT JOINs?
sk2.bodyMarkdown: LEFT and RIGHT JOINs are useful when you want to include all records from one table, even if there are no corresponding matches in the other table. For example, you might want to see all products, including those that have never been ordered, or all students, including those who have not enrolled in any classes.

q1.title: Understanding LEFT JOIN
q1.prompt: Which statement best describes what a LEFT JOIN does?
q1.hint: Think about which table's rows are always included.

help.concept: A LEFT JOIN includes all rows from the left table, even if there are no matches in the right table.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN keeps all rows from the left table, filling in NULLs if there's no match in the right table.
help.hint_2: The left table is always fully represented in the result, regardless of matches.

options.a: Returns all rows from the left table and matching rows from the right table.
options.b: Returns only rows that have matches in both tables.
options.c: Returns all rows from the right table and matching rows from the left table.
options.d: Returns only rows from the left table that do not have matches in the right table.

q2.title: RIGHT JOIN scenario
q2.prompt: If you want to list all employees, including those who have not been assigned to any project, which JOIN should you use if 'employees' is the right table?
q2.hint: Which JOIN includes all rows from the right table?

help.concept: A RIGHT JOIN includes all rows from the right table, even if there are no matches in the left table.
help.hint_1: RIGHT JOIN ensures every row from the right table appears in the result.
help.hint_2: If 'employees' is the right table, RIGHT JOIN will include all employees.

q3.title: LEFT vs RIGHT JOIN outcomes
q3.prompt: Select all statements that are true about LEFT and RIGHT JOINs.
q3.hint: Think about which table's rows are always included and when NULLs appear.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN includes all rows from the left table, RIGHT JOIN includes all from the right. NULLs appear where there are no matches.
help.hint_1: LEFT JOIN: all left rows, possible NULLs on right. RIGHT JOIN: all right rows, possible NULLs on left.
help.hint_2: Both JOINs can produce NULLs for unmatched rows, but always include all rows from one table.

options.a: LEFT JOIN can return rows with NULLs from the right table.
options.b: RIGHT JOIN can return rows with NULLs from the left table.
options.c: Both JOINs only return rows where there is a match in both tables.
options.d: LEFT JOIN always returns all rows from the left table.

q4.title: Order of JOIN results
q4.prompt: Arrange the steps of how a LEFT JOIN works.
q4.hint: Think about which table is the starting point.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN starts with the left table, matches rows, and fills NULLs for unmatched right table rows.
help.hint_1: First, gather all left table rows, then try to match with the right table.
help.hint_2: After matching, unmatched left rows get NULLs for right table columns.

tokens.t1: Start with all rows from the left table.
tokens.t2: Match rows from the right table based on the join condition.
tokens.t3: Include matching rows from the right table.
tokens.t4: For left table rows with no match, fill right table columns with NULL.

q5.title: Fill in the blank: LEFT JOIN
q5.prompt: A LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the ____ table and matching rows from the other table.
q5.hint: Which table's rows are always included in a the missing term JOIN?

help.concept: the missing term JOIN always includes all rows from the the missing term table.
help.hint_1: The answer is the table named in the JOIN type.
help.hint_2: the missing term JOIN = all rows from the the missing term table.

q5.template: A LEFT JOIN returns all rows from the [____] table and matching rows from the other table.

q6.title: Write a LEFT JOIN query
q6.prompt: Using the 'products_catalog' dataset, write a SQL query to list all products and their category names, including products that do not have a category.
q6.hint: Use LEFT JOIN to include all products, even those without a category.

help.concept: LEFT JOIN lets you see all rows from the left table, with NULLs for unmatched right table rows.
help.hint_1: Start with products, LEFT JOIN to categories using the category_id.
help.hint_2: Select product name and category name, joining on category_id = id.

q6.starterCode:
SELECT ... FROM products
LEFT JOIN categories ON ...;
